title: Run a LoRA hyperparameter sweep
description: Steps to sweep rank, target modules, and learning rate against a frozen eval to find a deployable adapter.
body: Use when tuning LoRA settings for a task. Do not pick rank and learning rate by intuition — the optimum varies by task and base. Actions: fix the eval, sweep rank, target modules, and learning rate over a small grid, score each adapter against the base on task and capability holdouts, and select the best inside the capability constraint. Validation signals include a sweep table ranking configs and a chosen adapter beating the base without a capability regression. Risk boundary: a config that wins on task but drops capability is not deployable; if every config regresses the holdout, escalate to revisit the data or method rather than ship.
